name: Kettlebell Overhead Press
resistance_profile: Single-arm strict press with the bell resting on the back of the forearm; the offset load demands rack stability and challenges the delt hardest through the mid-range sticking point.
cues: Keep the wrist straight and the bell on the forearm, not in the hand
Brace the whole trunk before pressing
Press the bell back so it finishes over the shoulder
execution_steps: Clean or curl a kettlebell into the rack position: bell resting on the back of the forearm, wrist straight, elbow tucked to the ribs.
Stand tall, brace the abs and glutes, and set the shoulder blade down.
Press the bell straight up, letting the wrist rotate so the palm faces forward at the top.
Finish with the biceps by the ear and the bell stacked over the shoulder and mid-foot.
Lower under control back to the rack position and reset before the next rep.
good_when: Training at home or travelling with a single kettlebell
You want to fix a strength imbalance between sides
You want core and shoulder stability trained alongside pressing
bad_when: You are chasing maximum front-delt load and have barbells available
Wrist or elbow pain in the kettlebell rack position
You cannot yet stabilise an offset overhead load
common_errors: Letting the wrist bend back under the bell
Leaning away from the bell to cheat it up
Flaring the ribs and overarching the lower back
regressions: Kettlebell push press using leg drive
Two-hand dumbbell or machine shoulder press
